title: amphilophium paniculatum
intro: Amphilophium paniculatum, also known as the yellow passionflower or passion vine, is a flowering plant species in the family Apocynaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Amphilophium paniculatum include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a wound healer and antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are commonly used in folk medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
